name: Housing Resources, Incorporated
description_short: Wisconsin non-profit providing free homebuyer education, counseling, and mortgage assistance to low- and moderate-income families across seven counties since 1991.
description_long:
Housing Resources, Inc. (HRI) was founded in 1991 in Milwaukee to address the homeownership gap for low- and moderate-income families in Wisconsin. The organization has spent over 30 years building expertise in housing counseling, financial empowerment, and homeownership education across Milwaukee, Waukesha, Ozaukee, Washington, Racine, Kenosha, and Walworth counties. As a non-profit, HRI operates without profit motive, focusing entirely on community stabilization and neighborhood improvement through expanded homeownership.

HRI offers comprehensive services for both prospective and current homeowners. For home buyers, they provide free educational workshops, one-on-one pre-purchase counseling with experienced specialists, credit report services, and assistance applying for down payment and closing cost grants (including programs like Downpayment Plus®, Milwaukee Home Down Payment Program, and Chase Homebuyer Grants). For existing homeowners, HRI offers post-purchase counseling, default/foreclosure counseling, reverse mortgage counseling, a tool loan program, minor home repair assistance, and mortgage assistance through county and municipal programs to prevent foreclosure.

HRI distinguishes itself through its deep local integration and specialized expertise. They operate the Racine Financial Empowerment Center and partner with established entities like Wintrust and Chase. Their one-on-one counseling covers the entire homebuying journey—from credit assessment and budgeting to loan closing—demonstrating thorough, individualized support rather than generic guidance. They explicitly serve low- and moderate-income families and provide services free or at minimal cost, removing financial barriers to housing education.

The primary limitation is geographic scope: HRI serves only seven Wisconsin counties, making it inaccessible to consumers outside this region. While their website lists extensive services, specific details about eligibility requirements, wait times, and availability are limited. The organization's reliance on grant programs means assistance depends on external funding and may have annual caps or competitive qualification criteria not fully detailed online.
pros: Free homebuyer education classes covering critical topics like credit, mortgage selection, and affordability assessment
Personalized one-on-one pre-purchase counseling with detailed action plans for first-time homebuyers
Assistance accessing 10+ down payment and closing cost grant programs, potentially eliminating major purchase barriers
Foreclosure prevention services including default counseling and mortgage assistance for existing homeowners
Established 30+ year track record as a trusted non-profit in Wisconsin with strong community partnerships
Post-purchase support including homeowner workshops and reverse mortgage counseling for long-term stability
Comprehensive services addressing credit correction, budgeting, loan selection, and insurance planning
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to seven Wisconsin counties; unavailable outside this region
Website lacks specific details on grant eligibility criteria, award amounts, processing timelines, and success rates
No clear information about counselor certifications, availability, or typical wait times for one-on-one sessions
Tool loan program and minor home repair services mentioned but not detailed regarding scope, coverage, or enrollment process
Limited transparency about mortgage assistance program specific requirements and funding availability
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Wisconsin seeking free pre-purchase education and grant assistance
Low- and moderate-income families attempting to improve credit and build down payment savings
Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage default seeking counseling and assistance programs
Individuals needing post-purchase financial management and homeowner education to sustain ownership
diagnosis: Housing Resources, Inc. is best for low- and moderate-income homebuyers in seven Wisconsin counties seeking free, comprehensive education and grant assistance to purchase their first home, as well as existing homeowners needing foreclosure prevention support. The main caveat is geographic limitation—the organization only serves Milwaukee, Waukesha, Ozaukee, Washington, Racine, Kenosha, and Walworth counties, making it inaccessible to consumers outside Wisconsin.
services: Free home buyer classes and monthly workshops
One-on-one pre-purchase counseling and action planning
Down payment and closing cost grant application assistance
Credit report review and credit correction guidance
Mortgage assistance programs (Milwaukee County, City of Racine, Waukesha County)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Wisconsin nonprofit providing free homebuyer education, counseling, and mortgage assistance across seven counties.
